notes: Bishop of Rome (pope) who died in 253, known for his firm and devout leadership in early Christianity.

docs.title: Letter CXXXIX
docs.author: Marcus Tullius Cicero
docs.excerpt: … not say a word to Cornelius, who is responsible for your unwise conduct, since you profess to have learnt legal wisdom from him. Rather press on your opportunity and the means put into your hands, than which none better will ever be found.

docs.title: Letter LX
docs.author: Jerome
docs.excerpt: 10. Still we may approve these things as the swathing bands of an infant faith. He who has been a loyal soldier under a strange banner is sure to deserve the laurel when he comes to serve his own king.

docs.title: Letter LXXIX
docs.author: Jerome
docs.excerpt: … the band called the Italian band, a devout man and one that feared God with all his house, which gave much alms to the people, and prayed to God alway." All this that is said of him I claim--with a change of name only--for my dear Nebridius.
